user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: China's state rescue of AI stocks vindicates HSBC's H2 volatility call — and raises the August stakes
talking_points.context_html: Following the AI-driven market volatility HSBC flagged in its H2 outlook and the Kimi K3 shock we covered this week, Beijing has now mobilised its broadest state market intervention in years — record ETF inflows, a roughly CNY60bn state capital injection and insurer buying — to stop the AI/semiconductor selloff from becoming a wider confidence crisis. For Adam, this is a live test of how fast policy support can absorb an AI-driven shock to HSBC's heavily China/HK-exposed wealth book ahead of the 4 August interims.

talking_points.headline: JPMorgan's Dimon: AI cut 30-40% of jobs in some units — but margins won't follow
talking_points.context_html: Jamie Dimon, CEO of JPMorgan Chase disclosed AI has already eliminated 30-40% of headcount in some units, but warned the savings won't flow to shareholders because rivals capture similar efficiency gains from the same technology. It's the sharpest test case yet of the AI-ROI question Adam's change execution team will face when HSBC reports on 4 August — headcount cuts alone won't move the market if every competitor matches them.
source_links: Dimon: AI Cut 30-40% of Jobs in Some Units

talking_points.headline: Google's cheaper Gemini 3.6 Flash targets the exact agentic-cost problem enterprise AI is hitting
talking_points.context_html: Google DeepMind's new Flash-tier models cut output-token usage by up to 17% and are purpose-built for multi-step agentic workflows, directly answering enterprise complaints about the cost of running agents at scale. HSBC's Google Cloud/Gemini partnership already targets 200+ AI use cases across wealth management and RM tooling; a materially cheaper agentic tier improves the unit economics of scaling those past pilot stage.
source_links: Google Releases Three New Gemini Models

Regulatory & Markets.title: China mounts broadest state market rescue in years to halt AI-driven tech stock selloff
Regulatory & Markets.summary: Beijing coordinated regulators, state-backed investors and insurers -- including a roughly CNY60bn injection from two state capital operators and record inflows into tech ETFs -- to stop a selloff in AI and semiconductor stocks from becoming a broader confidence crisis, days after Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 release rattled markets.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Dimon: AI has already cut 30-40% of headcount in some JPMorgan units, but won't lift margins
Competitor Intelligence.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon disclosed AI has eliminated 30-40% of headcount in some bank units, part of a roughly $20bn tech budget and nearly 1,000 live AI use cases, but warned banks 'don't uniquely benefit from AI' since rivals capture similar cost reductions, leaving relative margins largely unchanged.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Singapore banks' wealth income resilience cited as DBS, OCBC, UOB hold near record highs
Competitor Intelligence.summary: A SIAS weekly review flagged Singapore's three banks continuing to outperform on stronger wealth management fee income and higher-for-longer rate expectations, even as Middle East tensions push up oil prices and bond yields.

AI & Technology.title: Google releases Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite and 3.5 Flash Cyber for cheaper agentic workloads
AI & Technology.summary: Google DeepMind shipped three new Flash-tier models -- Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite and a cybersecurity-tuned 3.5 Flash Cyber -- cutting output-token usage by up to 17% and pricing the flagship at $1.50/$7.50 per million input/output tokens, targeting cheaper, faster multi-step agentic workflows.
